job_title.position: 27th President of the United States & 10th Chief Justice of the U.S.
job_title.role: President, Chief Justice, Secretary of War, Governor-General of the Philippines

resume.summary: William H. Taft was the **27th President of the United States (1909-1913)** and later became the **10th Chief Justice of the U.S. Supreme Court (1921-1930)**, the only person to hold both offices. His presidency focused on **trust-busting, civil service reform, and economic conservatism**, but he struggled politically due to his break with Theodore Roosevelt, leading to the split of the Republican Party.

Work_Experience.job_description: Led **progressive-era policies** focusing on **trust-busting, economic reform, and civil service expansion**. Advocated for **judicial independence**, though his presidency saw the Republican Party divide.
The **only former U.S. President to serve as Chief Justice**. Worked on **modernizing the federal judiciary** and improved Supreme Court efficiency.
Oversaw **military infrastructure**, led **Panama Canal administration**, and managed **diplomatic efforts in East Asia**.
Led **post-Spanish-American War governance**, implemented **civil reforms**, and promoted **economic development** in the Philippines.

notes: Taft's **legal expertise and judicial temperament** shaped his presidency, but his **rift with Theodore Roosevelt** led to the **Republican Party's split** in 1912. His post-presidential career as **Chief Justice** was his **true passion**, where he **left a lasting mark on the U.S. legal system**.
